creator: vvd@FreeBSD.org
text:
Description of my steps:

# cd /usr/ports/multimedia/mplayer/
# make config
- Remove SMB support.
# portmaster mplayer*
- Compiled OK.
# make config
- Add SMB support.
# portmaster mplayer*
- Error find X11 libs.
- But if I do:
# cd /usr/ports/net/samba-libsmbclient/
# make config
- Remove ADS support.
# portmaster libsmbclient*
# cd /usr/ports/multimedia/mplayer/
# make config
- Add SMB support.
# portmaster mplayer*
- Compiled OK.

With my patch mplayer compiled normal and with libsmbclient with ADS
support.

IMHO, openssl form ports incorectly detected by other ports.
One more example: net-im/psi - configure and compile runing with openssl
includes from ports (/usr/local/include), but linking with system libssl.so
(/usr/lib/libssl.so.5) - can't compile without patches for configure
scripts.

FreeBSD 7.1-STABLE i386 (csup ~1 weeks ago).
I can to send or upload to http "configure.log" and
"/var/db/ports/*/options" files for all ports (mplayer, libsmbclient,
openssl, psi, etc...).


P.S. Sorry for my english.



-----Original Message-----
From: Stefan Walter [mailto:stefan@freebsd.org] 
Sent: Tuesday, February 03, 2009 3:45 PM
To: Thomas Zander
Cc: Peter; VVD; GNATS
Subject: Re: ports/124423: multimedia/mplayer detection of OSS Audio
isfaulty


VVD,

can you please explain in detail how you try to install the mplayer port
when it fails, i.e. make flags/options etc.? Thomas wasn't able to reproduce
the failure you reported, yet, and the same is true for me.

Regards,
Stefan
